page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 29.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 0.9 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU test and Geekbench single-core score, fall into the bottom 25% tier. However, the 32-vCPU allocation elevates multi-core performance to average levels, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 23,469.51 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8,521.0. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle 50% tier, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 1,289.7 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 43.85 GB/sec.

Given the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak single-threaded performance, this server is less suited for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded tasks like gzip compression, which registers in the bottom 10%. Instead, it is optimized for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and static web serving, where the 32 vCPUs can be fully leveraged.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 29.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. Lacking integrated local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance allocates a low memory-to-core ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per core. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 23,469.51 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8,521.0. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, while single-threaded tasks and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. This hardware profile is best suited for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads with modest memory requirements, such as multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 29.0 GB of RAM, offering average multi-core performance for parallelized, compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 features 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 29.0 GB of RAM, optimized for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-vCPU, low-memory compute instance designed for parallelized workloads, multi-threaded compilation, and distributed processing tasks without GPU requirements.

29.0 GB of system memory providing 0.9 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average multi-core performance but weak single-core execution
Best suited for multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving
